title: Holiday Grilled Corn Recipes: Christmas, Easter
meta_description: Explore festive holiday grilled corn recipes for Christmas and Easter. Plus, the perfect tool to keep your hands safe!
html:
Grilled corn is a holiday crowd-pleaser, and with the right tools, it’s easier than ever to make. These holiday grilled corn recipes are perfect for Christmas gatherings or Easter brunches, adding a smoky, sweet touch to your menu. Whether you’re grilling classic buttery corn or spicing things up with herbs and seasonings, the Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders make handling hot cobs a breeze. Let’s dive into recipes and tips for festive grilling success.

Why Grilled Corn Is a Holiday Staple

Grilled corn on the cob is a versatile side that pairs with almost anything—roasted meats, seafood, or even veggie-centric dishes. It’s quick to prepare, adaptable to dietary preferences (gluten-free, vegan-friendly), and brings a nostalgic, smoky flavor to the table. Plus, it’s a hands-on activity for guests, making it a hit at backyard barbecues or holiday feasts.

Holiday Grilled Corn Recipes for Christmas and Easter

Here are two seasonal recipes to inspire you:

Classic Christmas Grilled Corn with Herb Butter

Ingredients:

  • 6-8 ears of corn, husks removed
  • 2 tbsp unsalted butter, softened
  • 1 tsp fresh parsley, chopped
  • 1/2 tsp garlic powder
  • 1/2 tsp paprika
  • Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

  1. Preheat grill to medium-high heat (375°F).
  2. Brush corn with melted butter mixed with herbs, salt, and pepper.
  3. Skewer cobs with the Girla Corn Holders to prevent burns.
  4. Grill for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally until charred.

Spicy Holiday Corn for a Festive Twist

For a kick, mix softened butter with 1 tsp cayenne pepper, 1 tbsp honey, and 1 tsp lime juice. Brush onto corn before grilling. Serve with a sprinkle of cilantro and a wedge of lime.

Easter Grilled Corn: Light and Spring-Fresh

Lighten things up for Easter with this lemon-herb version:

  • Replace butter with olive oil.
  • Add lemon zest and dill.
  • Serve with a drizzle of balsamic glaze.

The Girla Interlocking Holders make it easy to flip cobs without burning your hands, especially when serving a large crowd.

Simplify Grilling with the Right Tools

The Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders are a game-changer. The silicone handles stay cool, and the interlocking design saves space in your kitchen. Use them year-round for grilling meats, veggies, or even marshmallows for s’mores. Just toss them in the dishwasher after use!

How to Serve Holiday Grilled Corn

Beyond the plate, grilled corn can be:

  • Added to salads for crunch.
  • Stuffed with quinoa or beans for a main dish.
  • Used as a colorful garnish for cocktails.

FAQ

How do I prevent grilled corn from burning?

Use the Girla Corn Holders to hold cobs upright and away from direct flames. Rotate occasionally for even cooking.

Can I grill corn without removing the husks?

Yes! Wrap husked corn in foil and grill for 15-20 minutes for a subtler smoky flavor.

Are the corn holders safe for indoor use?

Absolutely—these holders work on stovetops, griddles, or outdoor grills. Heat-resistant silicone handles make them versatile all year.
